props_visible: Long wooden dining table with ~20+ place settings
White folded napkins on each setting
Fringed pendant lights on rope suspension (natural fiber/jute)
Wooden wall panels with colorful circular ceramic inlay ornaments (rows of dots in multiple colors)
notable_visual_elements: Linear perspective creates dramatic depth — the table appears to stretch endlessly
Colorful ceramic dot ornaments in wooden panels = Najdi heritage craft motif (mashrabiya/decorative elements)
Rope-hung pendant lights = traditional rope/nomadic material in contemporary fixture
Gold woven placemats echo traditional Saudi embroidery/craft aesthetic
No food or guests = pure atmosphere content

hospitality_cues: Long communal table = Saudi tradition of hosting large family groups together
20+ place settings signal that generosity of scale is a feature not just capacity
cultural_notes.free_notes: The communal long table is a powerful Saudi cultural signal — eating together as a large group is central to Najdi hospitality. The space design mixes heritage materials (natural rope, woven fiber, ceramic craft ornaments) with contemporary architecture (clean lines, tall windows, modern table forms). This is the visual language of 'luxury Najdi' — heritage craft elevated to premium dining.

pattern_matches.notes: Linear perspective table shot — classic restaurant interior architecture composition
Empty-table hospitality staging shows the full promise of the experience before guests arrive
